title: Packaging Corporation Of America Is A Long Term Buy
text: Here at the Lab, we provide a five-year analysis versus IP and WestRock. PKG shares the same long-term thesis with a more solid balance sheet and safer shareholder remuneration.

title: Packaging Corporation of America Schedules Conference Call to Discuss Third Quarter 2022 Operating Results
text: LAKE FOREST, Ill.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE: PKG) will hold a conference call on Tuesday, October 25, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss third quarter 2022 results. The conference call leader will be Mark Kowlzan. Third quarter earnings results will be released after the market closes on Monday, October 24, 2022. To access the conference call, you may pre-register for the conference by navigating to http://dpregister.com/sreg/10170634/f42801ac8a. Once you have registered, you will receive your dial-in number. When dialing in the day of the call, please ask to join the Packaging Corporation of America earnings call. If you prefer not to pre-register, you may dial in the day of the call using these toll free numbers: 866-777-2509 (U.S.), 866-605-3852 (Canada) or 412-317-5413 (International) by 8:45 a.m. (Eastern Time). Please ask to join the Packaging Corporation of America earnings call. A replay of the call will also be available from October 25, 2022 until November 8, 2022. To access the recording, please dial toll free 877-344-7529 (U.S.), 855-669-9658 (Canada) or (412) 317-0088 (International). The replay Access Code is: 8761960 This call will also be webcast and accessible at PCA’s website at www.packagingcorp.com. Please go to the Investor Relations tab/Current Events. PCA is the third largest producer of containerboard products and a leading producer of uncoated freesheet paper in North America. PCA operates eight paper mills and 90 corrugated products plants and related facilities.

title: FedEx Warning Drags Down Amazon and Other E-Commerce, Packaging Shares
text: Some packaging stocks were on track for their worst day since 2020 following FedEx's worse-than-expected earnings and withdrawn guidance.

title: Why International Paper, WestRock, and Packaging Corp. Stocks All Crashed Today
text: Paper companies look cheap -- and as it turns out, there's a reason for that.

title: 3 Packaging Stocks With Modest Dividend Yield to Watch
text: Strong demand will support packaging stocks despite high costs and supply-chain headwinds. PKG, SON and GEF are some high-quality dividend stocks that can fetch investors promising returns.

title: 3 High-Quality and Undervalued Names Paying Market-Beating Yields
text: The S&P 500 Index is close to bear market territory. As a result, most sectors are down for the year.
